editableNewDialogue.text: Can you help me polish this sentence so it sounds more formal: "The results were good but the timeline was not."

Sure—here are a few formal options:
1) “The outcomes were positive; however, the timeline was not optimal.”
2) “The results were satisfactory, but the schedule was less favorable.”
3) “Although the results were strong, the project timeline did not meet expectations.”
Which one is best for an academic statement of purpose?
Option 3 fits best for a statement of purpose because it’s clear and objective: “Although the results were strong, the project timeline did not meet expectations.”
Can you make it slightly less negative?

“Yes—here are two softer versions:
- “Although the results were strong, the project timeline was longer than initially planned.”
- “The results were strong, and the project ultimately required more time than expected.”
By the way, I read a short piece saying the Voyager probes are still sending data. Is that true?

Yes. Voyager 1 and Voyager 2 are still operating, though with limited instruments as their power supply declines. NASA has been turning off some systems to conserve energy, but they continue to transmit engineering and some scientific data back to Earth.
Interesting—also, what’s a quick example of a polite follow-up email after no reply?

Subject: Follow-up on [Topic]

Dear Dr. [Name],

I hope you’re doing well. I’m writing to follow up on my email below regarding [topic]. If you had a chance to review it, I would be grateful for any updates when convenient.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
fix "When I was searching professors’ papers, Dr. Margala’s work on VLSI design was exactly close to what I had in mind, so I kept following his publications and then emailed him."
When I was reviewing professors’ papers, Dr. Margala’s work on VLSI design aligned closely with what I had in mind. That’s why I continued following his publications and later emailed him.
fix "My thesis was interrupted because my pregnancy was high-risk, and then childcare and Corona delayed everything."

My thesis timeline was affected by pregnancy and family responsibilities, as well as the COVID-19 disruption, but it wasn’t described as a high-risk pregnancy. I was advised to rest, yet I kept working until I started bleeding and then I paused the thesis temporarily.
Yes, exactly—I was told to rest, I continued working, then I started bleeding and stopped for a while; after the birth I was busy with my children, and later Corona also slowed things down.
